nativeRange.habitat: Marine reef-associated; coral heads on shallow tropical reefs.
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical western Atlantic coral reefs, typically at 1-40 m depth.

diet.notes: Accepts a wide range of prepared and frozen foods; generally takes frozen mysis and brine shrimp readily, along with pellets and frozen carnivore preparations. Wild fish supplement their diet by picking ectoparasites from larger reef fish.

avoidWith: conspecifics other than a mated pair

summary: Small western-Atlantic cleaner goby that picks parasites off larger reef fish. Generally considered reef-safe and beginner-friendly; tends to do well singly or as a mated pair.
careNotes: Often marketed as both 'Neon Goby' and 'Neon Blue Goby' (Elacatinus oceanops). Recognised by an iridescent blue stripe running along a near-black body, it is one of the classic Caribbean cleaner gobies and has been documented servicing groupers, snappers, and other reef fish. Generally hardy and reef-safe; reported to ignore corals and ornamental invertebrates. Best kept singly or as a bonded pair, as conspecifics tend to fight unless paired. Captive spawning has been reported, with eggs laid in crevices or empty shells. Has been reported to live around 1-2 years, which is short by aquarium standards; longer captive lifespans (up to ~3 years) have occasionally been noted. Cross-source check: LiveAquaria gives a 10-gallon (~38 L) minimum which is widely echoed by hobby sources, but the project's tank-size convention rounds up to a nicer, more long-term value (75 L) — comfortably accommodates a pair plus rockwork on a small reef setup.
breedingNotes: Has been bred in home aquariums. Forms monogamous pairs and tends to spawn on the underside of overhangs, in crevices, or inside empty shells. Male typically guards the eggs.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source: tank size, water parameters, temperament, diet, reef-safe status.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, taxonomy, native range, maximum length (5.0 cm TL), preferred temperature range (24.5-28°C, observed 21-28°C), monogamous mating, cleaner-fish ecology. Used to average the temperature range with LiveAquaria's 22.2-25.6°C.

fish.breedingNotes: Forms monogamous pairs; spawns in crevices or shells. Larvae rearing is the main hurdle.
